findings.description: SQL injection: the login query is built via f-string interpolating the attacker-controlled 'email' parameter directly into SQL (e.g. email="' OR '1'='1' -- "), enabling auth bypass and arbitrary data exfiltration (CWE-89 / OWASP A03:2021 Injection); fix by using a parameterized query: conn.execute("SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = ? AND is_active = 1", (email,)).

Inverted session-expiry check: 'if session.expires_at > datetime.utcnow()' deletes still-valid sessions and returns None, while expired sessions fall through to 'return session.user_id', so expired tokens are accepted indefinitely and the TTL is defeated (CWE-613 Insufficient Session Expiration); invert to 'if session.expires_at <= datetime.utcnow(): del ...; return None'.

Account-lockout denial of service: failed_attempts is only reset on a successful login (line 82) but the lockout check (line 70) precedes the password check, so once an account reaches MAX_FAILED_ATTEMPTS it is permanently locked with no time-based unlock — the declared LOCKOUT_MINUTES constant is never used (CWE-645); implement a timestamped lockout that auto-expires after LOCKOUT_MINUTES.

Username/account enumeration via timing side-channel: login returns early for unknown emails (and for locked accounts at line 70) before invoking the expensive 100k-iteration pbkdf2_hmac at line 73, so valid emails respond measurably slower; perform a dummy hash on the not-found/locked paths to equalize timing.

Resource-exhaustion / DoS smell: find_user_by_email runs 'SELECT * FROM users' loading the entire users table into memory and linear-scanning it on every call, which degrades and can exhaust memory as the table grows (CWE-400); use an indexed 'WHERE email = ?' parameterized lookup instead (primarily a performance concern, deferred to the performance critic).

Scan flagged log injection here and at line 107, but both log only integer DB columns (user.id / session.user_id) via the logging module's lazy '%s' parameterization (not f-strings), so there is no CRLF/newline injection vector and no format-string risk; reported for completeness but effectively non-exploitable as written.
